name: Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop
name_original: Miravinya la Cadira y monte Cúgol — circular desde Sant Pere de Riudebitlles
description: Jog the moderate Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop, a 6.3-mile route with vineyard views and the Miravinya La Cadira viewpoint.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sant Pere De Riudebitlles traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, vineyards, and river valleys. The region offers a mix of terrain, from gentle paths alongside streams to routes with moderate elevation changes through agricultural areas. This varied topography provides diverse options for runners seeking different challenges and scenic backdrops. Best jogging routes around Sant Pere De Riudebitlles The most popular jogging route is Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of the Penedès wine region. Another top favourite among local runners is Camí del Riu Trail, an easy 2.8 miles (4.5 km) path. This trail follows the river, providing a flat and accessible running experience. Local runners also love the Crossing of the Rovira stream loop from el Pla del Penedès, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through stream valleys and agricultural fields, often completed in about 40 minutes. Jogging around Sant Pere De Riudebitlles is defined by vineyards, river paths, and gentle hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult running routes. The routes in Sant Pere De Riudebitlles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Sant Pere De Riudebitlles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Sant Pere De Riudebitlles?
A: There are over 70 running routes in the Sant Pere De Riudebitlles area, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels. You'll find 7 easy routes, 48 moderate, and 15 more challenging trails.

Q: Are there easy running paths suitable for beginners in Sant Pere De Riudebitlles?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy running paths perfect for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131572810/cami-del-riu-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí del Riu Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an accessible 2.8-mile (4.5 km) path that follows the river, providing a flat and pleasant running experience.

Q: Where can I find longer running trails around Sant Pere De Riudebitlles?
A: For those seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1132041678/penedes-360-trekking-la-cadira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This moderate 6.3-mile (10.1 km) trail offers panoramic views of the Penedès wine region and takes about 1 hour 12 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes in this area?
A: The running routes around Sant Pere De Riudebitlles feature a varied landscape. You'll encounter rolling hills, extensive vineyards, and serene river valleys. The terrain ranges from gentle paths alongside streams to routes with moderate elevation changes through agricultural fields, providing diverse options for all runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sant Pere De Riudebitlles?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10430351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossing of the Rovira stream loop from el Pla del Penedès&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.9-mile (6.3 km) trail that leads through stream valleys and agricultural fields.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sant Pere De Riudebitlles?
A: The running routes in Sant Pere De Riudebitlles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the beautiful vineyard scenery, the peaceful river paths, and the variety of terrain that caters to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and natural beauty. While running, you might encounter sights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3312743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gran Pèlag of Vilobí del Penedès&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful lake, or enjoy views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravinya La Bardera&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features historical sites like various castles, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3934046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí Sarroca Castle and Church Complex&lt;/a&gt;, which are often nearby.

Q: Is public transport available to access the running trails?
A: Sant Pere De Riudebitlles is a smaller town in a rural setting. While local bus services connect it to larger towns, direct public transport access to specific trailheads might be limited. It's generally recommended to check local bus schedules in advance or consider driving to the starting points.

Q: Are there any running routes that start directly from Sant Pere de Riudebitlles?
A: Yes, there are routes that begin directly from the town. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23776359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sant Pere de Riudebitlles&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.8-mile (6.08 km) trail that offers a convenient starting point for local runners.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Sant Pere De Riudebitlles?
A: The Penedès region, including Sant Pere De Riudebitlles, generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are ideal for running, offering mild temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any running routes near Torrelavit?
A: Yes, you can find running options near the neighboring village of Torrelavit. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6936153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Torrelavit&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.7-mile (9.27 km) route that explores the surrounding countryside.
tips: A giant chair stands on the small hill of Malgranada (Torrelavit), an ideal place from which to interpret nature, with a 360-degree view. The chair invites you to stop and contemplate, to sit and listen in the middle of a privileged landscape, the Penedés.
Set of agricultural fields interspersed with the pine forests of the area and found in this area of hills near Lavit. Area full of lanes and paths to get lost in!
Track between fields of olive trees that takes you up to the hill of Puig Cúgol and which rises above three hundred and fifty meters in height.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated jogging time for this 10 km loop is approximately 72 minutes (1 hour and 12 minutes). This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is the La Cadira Loop suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: The La Cadira Loop is rated as moderate. While it's a manageable distance at 10 km, it includes an elevation gain of about 186 meters. The varied terrain, which can include dirt tracks and gentle hills, requires good fitness and sure-footedness. It might be challenging for absolute beginners but is suitable for those with some jogging experience.

faqs.question: Where does the Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead isn't detailed, the loop is centered around the area of Miravinya La Cadira. As it's a circular route, you can typically start at various points. For parking, look for available spots in nearby towns or designated areas close to the viewpoint, though specific parking facilities for this exact jogging loop are not explicitly mentioned.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Cadira Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain generally consists of varied landscapes, including sections through vineyards and forests. You can expect dirt tracks and paths, some of which may be natural 'albero' surfaces. While paths are often easily accessible, some moderate sections may require sure-footedness due to gentle hills and varied ground conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: This loop offers picturesque views characteristic of the Penedès region. You'll pass through extensive vineyards, which change beautifully with the seasons. A major highlight is the Miravinya La Cadira (The Chair Viewpoint), providing panoramic vistas. You'll also encounter the Perico Vineyards and experience the Puig Cúgol climb/ascent, offering diverse natural beauty.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the La Cadira Loop?
faqs.answer: The Penedès region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are generally ideal for jogging. In spring, the vineyards are lush and green, while autumn brings vibrant colors as the leaves change. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if visiting during that season. Winters are typically mild.

faqs.question: Does the La Cadira Loop intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with other established paths. Notably, it covers portions of the Penedès 360 Trekking (44% of the route), Penedès 360 Slow Bike (38%), and Camí de la Coma (13%). This means you might encounter other hikers or cyclists on shared segments.

faqs.question: Is the La Cadira Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through vineyards or near agricultural areas. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the trail's cleanliness.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no known permits or entrance fees required to jog the Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, wear comfortable jogging shoes with good grip. Layered athletic clothing is advisable, as temperatures can change. Bring sufficient water, especially on warmer days, and consider a hat and sunscreen. A small backpack for essentials like snacks and your phone is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the La Cadira Loop?
faqs.answer: While the loop itself is primarily natural, the Penedès region is renowned for its gastronomy and viticulture. You'll find charming towns and wineries nearby where you can enjoy local cuisine, cafes, and, of course, wine tastings. It's a great opportunity to combine your jog with a cultural and culinary experience.
